title: Automated Trolley
description: Built a remote-controlled platform capable of carrying a payload of up to 50 kg.
highlights: Collaborated with a multidisciplinary team in the successful development of a practical, automated trolley solution for industrial and manufacturing applications.
Employed SOLIDWORKS for the initial design phase of the automated trolley, ensuring precision and efficiency in the development process.
Addressed challenges in loading/unloading mechanisms, straight-line movement, and 90° turns, showcasing a practical understanding of engineering applications.

title: TGGS - Motorbike Dolly for Crash Testing
description: Design of a motorbike dolly for crash testing with comprehensive engineering analysis.
highlights: Designed a motorbike crash-test dolly in SOLIDWORKS, developing the full design from initial concept sketches through to production-ready mechanical assemblies.
Performed iterative Finite Element Analysis (FEA) under simulated crash loading conditions, optimizing geometry to meet target safety factors while minimizing material usage.
Engineered an energy-absorbing crash cushion system, reducing peak impact forces on test equipment and improving repeatability of crash test results.

title: WEXTECH HK LIMITED - Product Designer
description: Professional product design role focusing on consumer products from concept to production.
highlights: Conceptualized and sketched initial design concepts, considering user preferences and market trends
Utilized CAD software to create detailed 3D models and prototypes
Successfully brought the designs from concept to production, including Seed Frame products

title: Cleaning Device for Elderly People
description: Designed an accessible cleaning device specifically for the elderly population of Hong Kong.
highlights: Conducted research with a team of seven to develop a user-friendly cleaning device for elderly residents in Hong Kong.
Utilized design tools including functional decomposition diagrams, mind maps, and concept sketches for ideation.
Developed a SOLIDWORKS prototype incorporating product architecture, component selection, and material optimization.
Presented the accessibility, ease of use, and effectiveness of the cleaning device to an audience.

title: Table Fan Redesign
description: Comprehensive redesign and optimization of a table fan with focus on performance and manufacturability.
highlights: Collaborated with a team of seven people to analyze and redesign table fan components for improved performance and manufacturability, based on analysis and market research.
Employed hand-drawn sketches and CAD models in SOLIDWORKS for detailed prototyping and stress simulation.
Assembled all components of the original fan in SOLIDWORKS, allowing for a comprehensive view of the product.

title: Gripper Design with Water Bottle
description: Designed a mechanical gripper system for bottle handling with detailed CAD modeling.
highlights: Created a volume-defined bottle design using SOLIDWORKS.
Enhanced gripper for efficient bottle grasping, employing mechanical design principles.
Generated renderings with varied appearances and utilized image mapping and animation videos to showcase the bottle's gripping mechanism.
Produced precise 2D drawings of components with title blocks and annotations.

title: Robot Arm Assembly
description: Designed and developed a six degrees of freedom robot arm using SOLIDWORKS.
highlights: Designed and developed six degrees of Freedom Robot Arm using SOLIDWORKS.
Designed the Cover, Base, Shoulder Support, Upper Arm Model, Power Cable, and the Server Motor Subassembly.
Assembled all the created parts along with the predesigned parts to create the Robot Arm.
